2603.25253.title: MolQuest: A Benchmark for Agentic Evaluation of Abductive Reasoning in Chemical Structure Elucidation
2603.25253.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) hold considerable potential for advancing scientific discovery, yet systematic assessment of their dynamic reasoning in real-world research remains limited. Current scientific evaluation benchmarks predominantly rely on static, single-turn Question Answering (QA) formats, which are inadequate for measuring model performance in complex scientific tasks that require multi-step iteration and experimental interaction. To address this gap, we introduce MolQuest, a novel agent-based evaluation framework for molecular structure elucidation built upon authentic chemical experimental data. Unlike existing datasets, MolQuest formalizes molecular structure elucidation as a multi-turn interactive task, requiring models to proactively plan experimental steps, integrate heterogeneous spectral sources (e.g., NMR, MS), and iteratively refine structural hypotheses. This framework systematically evaluates LLMs' abductive reasoning and strategic decision-making abilities within a vast and complex chemical space. Empirical results reveal that contemporary frontier models exhibit significant limitations in authentic scientific scenarios: notably, even state-of-the-art (SOTA) models achieve an accuracy of only approximately 50%, while the performance of most other models remains below the 30% threshold. This work provides a reproducible and extensible framework for science-oriented LLM evaluation, our findings highlight the critical gap in current LLMs' strategic scientific reasoning, setting a clear direction for future research toward AI that can actively participate in the scientific process.
2603.25253.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在推進科學發現方面具有相當大的潛力，但對其在現實研究中動態推理的系統評估仍然有限。<lb/>當前的科學評估基準主要依賴靜態的單輪問答（QA）格式，這對於測量模型在需要多步迭代和實驗互動的複雜科學任務中的表現是不足的。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們引入了MolQuest，一個基於真實化學實驗數據的分子結構闡明的新型代理評估框架。<lb/>與現有數據集不同，MolQuest將分子結構闡明形式化為一個多輪互動任務，要求模型主動計劃實驗步驟，整合異質光譜來源（例如，NMR、MS），並迭代地完善結構假設。<lb/>該框架系統地評估LLMs的溯因推理和戰略決策能力，在廣泛而複雜的化學空間中進行。<lb/>實證結果顯示，當代前沿模型在真實科學場景中表現出顯著的局限性：值得注意的是，即使是最先進的（SOTA）模型的準確率也僅約為50%，而大多數其他模型的表現則低於30%的門檻。<lb/>這項工作提供了一個可重複和可擴展的科學導向LLM評估框架，我們的發現突顯了當前LLMs在戰略科學推理方面的關鍵缺口，為未來研究朝向能夠積極參與科學過程的AI設定了明確的方向。
2603.25253.author: Taolin Han et.al.
2603.25253.authors: Taolin Han, Shuang Wu, Jinghang Wang, Yuhao Zhou, Renquan Lv, Bing Zhao, Wei Hu
